questions.text: What is the minimum sound frequency required for ultrasound?
Which term describes the bending of sound from a straight path as it passes from one medium to another?
For maximum reflection of an ultrasound beam, the angle of the interface to the transducer should be closest to:
Which structure in the transducer emits and receives the ultrasound waves?
Which two are the primary methods of echocardiography?
A standard echocardiographic examination begins in which transducer position?
For parasternal and apical echocardiographic views, the patient should be positioned in:
For subcostal and suprasternal notch views, the patient should be positioned in:
The parasternal short axis view at the basal level showing the open mitral valve leaflets is commonly called:
What is the starting landmark used to obtain apical echocardiographic views?

answers.text: The 4th intercostal space at the left sternal border

questions.text: The apical four-chamber view is most valuable for assessing:

answers.text: The relative sizes of the ventricles

questions.text: All three apical views are considered essential for analysis of:
The subcostal position is most useful in patients with:

answers.text: Obesity and a large chest wall
COPD or emphysema with hyperinflated lungs

questions.text: The primary use of the suprasternal echocardiographic view is to examine:
In M-Mode echocardiography, the vertical axis represents:
According to the BART color convention in color flow Doppler imaging, blue represents:
In color flow Doppler imaging, which color indicates turbulent flow?
A contrast (bubble) study uses agitated saline to primarily identify:
During a bubble study, bubbles that appear in the LEFT atrium shortly after entering the right atrium indicate:

answers.text: A normal finding — no shunt present
An atrial septal defect or patent foramen ovale

questions.text: The most common indication for stress echocardiography is evaluation of:

answers.text: Myocardial ischemia or coronary artery disease

questions.text: Which pharmacologic agent is most commonly used for pharmacologic stress echocardiography?
During a TEE procedure, the patient should be positioned in:

answers.text: Supine with the head of bed elevated 30°

questions.text: The primary indication for TEE is:

answers.text: Identification of source of embolism (from atrial fibrillation)
Measurement of right ventricular ejection fraction
Assessment of stress-induced wall motion abnormalities

questions.text: Which of the following is the most serious structural complication of TEE?
